task_completion.description: Verify the tag 'Landmark Decision' was created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the tag selector pane (usually in the bottom-left corner of the Jurism window). The tag 'Landmark Decision' (case-sensitive) should be visible in the list of tags.

task_completion.description: Verify a display color was assigned to the 'Landmark Decision' tag.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the tag selector pane, there should be a distinct colored square indicator immediately next to the 'Landmark Decision' tag, indicating a color has been assigned to it.

task_completion.description: Verify 'Brown v. Board of Education' is tagged with the colored tag.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the main library list view, the row for 'Brown v. Board of Education' should display the colored square indicator corresponding to the 'Landmark Decision' tag. Alternatively, if the item is selected, 'Landmark Decision' appears in its Tags tab.

task_completion.description: Verify 'Miranda v. Arizona' is tagged with the colored tag.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the main library list view, the row for 'Miranda v. Arizona' should display the colored square indicator corresponding to the 'Landmark Decision' tag. Alternatively, if the item is selected, 'Landmark Decision' appears in its Tags tab.

task_completion.description: Verify 'Gideon v. Wainwright' is tagged with the colored tag.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the main library list view, the row for 'Gideon v. Wainwright' should display the colored square indicator corresponding to the 'Landmark Decision' tag. Alternatively, if the item is selected, 'Landmark Decision' appears in its Tags tab.

task_completion.description: Verify that ONLY the three specified cases have the 'Landmark Decision' tag.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the main library list view. Only the rows for Brown, Miranda, and Gideon should have the colored square indicator. If the 'Landmark Decision' tag is selected in the tag pane to filter the view, exactly 3 items should be visible in the center pane.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Jurism graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Jurism application window with menus, dialogs, or right-click context menus open, rather than terminal windows executing sqlite3 commands to modify the database directly.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual Jurism application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface must be the standard Jurism/Zotero layout (three panes: collections, items, item details). It should not be a text editor, image editor, or a mocked interface.

integrity.description: Verify the agent tagged the pre-existing cases rather than creating dummy items.
integrity.visual_evidence: The tagged items should show correct metadata in the list view (e.g., years 1954, 1966, 1963) matching the real Supreme Court cases, rather than blank or newly created dummy entries.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target cases are: 1) Brown v. Board of Education, 347 U.S. 483 (1954); 2) Miranda v. Arizona, 384 U.S. 436 (1966); 3) Gideon v. Wainwright, 372 U.S. 335 (1963). The agent must tag exactly these 3 cases with 'Landmark Decision'. In Jurism, assigning a color to a tag makes a small colored square appear next to the tag in the tag pane, and the same colored square appears on the corresponding item rows in the main library view.
